content:
Each player checks their hand size at the end of the player phase, either discarding down to or drawing up to the number of cards indicated by their hand size value.

  • When drawing up to their hand size, a player draws cards one at a time, checking after each card is drawn whether they are at their hand size.

During the Deal Encounter Cards step of the villain phase, for each hazard icon on cards in play, deal one player one additional card (not one card per player). Additional cards are dealt in player order (first additional card to the first player, the second to the second player, etc.).

  • Each hazard icon is equivalent to the following constant ability: “During the Deal Encounter Cards step of the villain phase, deal 1 additional encounter card. Additional cards from hazard icons are dealt in player order.”

If an ability heals a character, damage the character has sustained can be removed from the character.

  • A heal effect can only bring a character to its maximum hit points, unless the effect explicitly states it can bring the character above its maximum.
  • Effects that move damage off of a character are considered to heal that character.

A card with the hinder X keyword enters play with X threat on it. (X is the value next to the hinder keyword.)

  • The hinder X keyword is equivalent to the following constant ability: “This card enters play with X threat on it.”
  • This threat is in addition to any threat it normally enters play with, such as a side scheme’s starting threat.

• Each character has a hit point value representing durability. 'Starting hit points' refers to an identity's printed value.
  • An identity's/villain's hit point dial represents remaining hit points; if reduced to zero, the player is defeated and eliminated, or that villain stage is defeated.
  • When an ability gives an identity/villain '+X hit points,' increase the dial by X (and reduce it by X when the effect ceases).
  • Damage on an ally/minion is tracked by damage tokens; zero or fewer remaining hit points defeats and discards it.
  • If a 'gets +X hit points' effect ceasing causes damage equal to/greater than hit points, that ally/minion is defeated.
  • Some characters may have infinite hit points and cannot be defeated by damage, though damage may still be dealt.
